content:
# Referral Limit Questions

## How The Rolling Limit Works
Northstar Bank applies a rolling referral limit across referral activity. A customer may have up to 2 qualifying referrals within any 9-day rolling window. The window is measured from the referral activity being reviewed, not from a calendar month or statement cycle. If the customer has already reached 2 referrals inside the active 9-day span, another referral should not be treated as eligible until older activity falls outside that span.

## Customer Answers
Explain the rule as a timing limit, not a permanent block. A customer who is at the limit can become eligible again when enough time has passed for prior referral activity to age out of the 9-day window. Do not quote a new date unless the prior referral dates are available in the customer record.

## Program References
Use this page only for the shared referral cap. See "Referral Limits And Eligibility" for broader eligibility handling, and see "Personal Checking Referral Programs" when the question is about the product-specific referral offer. Do not add program-specific reward amounts, account requirements, or exceptions unless they are documented in the applicable program article.
title: Referral Limit Questions
